posts.text: 4,857 Helium IoT map coordinates each host 10+ hotspots. Fleet≈1,008,034. Not a verdict—just a stack pattern worth a look. High-density points can be batch onboards, shared rooftops, or coordinate reuse. Checkable on the public map.

501 Helium IoT hotspots resolve to the exact same GPS point. This can come from default coords, templated installers, or indoor units using a site pin. Registry hygiene: flag per-coordinate counts and prompt updates on repeated exact matches.

Operators: how do you cap per-coordinate density without blocking legit shared sites? IoT shows 4,857 coords with 10+ hotspots; Mobile shows 529 coords with 10+. What heuristics (distance buckets, per-site attestations) work in practice?

Helium IoT team: any published policy on duplicate coordinates and per-site limits? Today’s read: 501 hotspots at one point; 4,857 coords hosting 10+. A brief note on expected behaviors and remediation would help downstream integrators.

thread: 501 Helium IoT hotspots sit on the exact same GPS coordinate. Another 4,857 coordinates each host 10+ hotspots. Fleet≈1,008,034. Pattern worth a look, not a verdict.
What it’s NOT: not price talk, not proof of abuse, not custody claims. Dense points can come from shared roofs, warehoused installs, or reused default pins.
What’s worth a look: per-coordinate caps, installer UX that prevents default coords, and map deduping. Helium Mobile shows a similar shape: 497 on one point; 529 coords with 10+.

Reproducible: pull the public hotspot list, group by exact lat/lon string. Count per coordinate. You’ll see 501 at one point on IoT and 4,857 coords with 10+. Same method on Mobile yields 497 and 529.
Daily integrity reads. Neutral, checkable. getkinetik.app
